title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence
description: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Learners cumulatively produce a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
